title: On Wednesdays We Use AI
subtitle: What AI is, why it matters, and how to try it without disappearing into a 40-hour course.
lesson: What AI is and how to have a first safe interaction with the major tools.
emotionalBeat: Private embarrassment becomes permission to start.
oneLineDescription: The one in which she realizes AI is already being added to the invisible load, says "ugh, as if," and opens the tab anyway.
episodeDescription: The one in which she opens the tab, realizes AI is not just for the fleece-vest section of the office, tries ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini, and rates the results on the butterfly clip scale instead of panic.
previously: This is the beginning. No previous episode, just the part where she stops waiting for a free weekend.
tryOn: Open ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Give each the same low-risk work explanation prompt. Compare the outputs and pick the one that feels most useful.
mainCharacterEnergy: I do not need to understand everything to try one useful thing.
rememberLine: You'll need to pour yourself more than a cup of ambition to keep up in the male-dominated world of AI - but lucky for you, this series comes in small sips. Small sips. Big moves.
communityPrompt: What AI question are you embarrassed to ask at work?

reelHooks: You are not behind on AI.
AI is not Google in a blazer.
Cher had a closet computer in 1995. You can open the tab.
I do not want to wait for our lives to be over before women learn AI.
social.carouselIdea: 5 tiny ways to try AI this week without taking a course.
social.storyPoll: Have you tried ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini for real work yet?
social.captionAngle: Do not wait for the AI skills gap to become another invisible load. Start with one low-risk prompt.
social.cta: Save this and send it to the friend who keeps saying she needs to learn AI.
